concepts.name: Scenario Implementation
concepts.definition: The work of translating scenario insights into strategy, decisions, communication, monitoring, and management routines rather than leaving scenarios as workshop artifacts.

concepts.name: Wind Tunneling
concepts.definition: Testing strategies, plans, or options across scenarios to reveal robustness, vulnerabilities, and contingent actions.

concepts.name: Strategic Conversation
concepts.definition: An ongoing organizational dialogue in which scenarios provide a shared language for uncertainty, assumptions, and possible action.

arguments.claim: Integration discipline separates scenario planning from scenario shelf-decoration.
arguments.evidence: Organizations often spend months constructing scenarios and then file them away; value appears only when scenarios affect decisions, monitoring, and capability building.

arguments.claim: Scenarios must become governance and cadence, not only stories.
arguments.evidence: Steering structures, champions, refresh cycles, executive briefings, cascading workshops, and trigger-based updates keep scenario thinking alive.

cases.name: Wind-tunneling strategic options
cases.context: A leadership team tests a proposed strategy against multiple plausible futures.
cases.illustrates: A strategy can look strong in one future and fragile in another, revealing hedges, signposts, and contingent moves.
cases.lesson: Use scenarios to improve choices, not merely to describe futures.

logicChains.description: Develop scenarios → test current strategy through wind tunneling → identify robust moves and vulnerabilities → define signposts → assign owners → refresh scenarios as triggers appear.

terminology.definition: Indicators that show which scenario or underlying force may be emerging.
Internal sponsor or practitioner who keeps scenario thinking connected to ongoing decisions.
A follow-on workshop that spreads scenario thinking beyond the original executive group.

nuance: Integration does not mean forcing one scenario to become the plan; it means making strategy robust across uncertainty.
Scenario refresh cadence should fit the volatility of the environment rather than follow ritual timing alone.
